SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.state

Interface StateStore

All Known Implementing Classes:
SQLiteStateStore
public interface StateStore
Persistence interface for the Smart Home system's coordinator state.

Abstracts over the underlying storage mechanism (SQLite, JSON file, in-memory) so that business logic in the coordinator and Automation Hub never depends on a specific database. The production implementation is SQLiteStateStore; an in-memory implementation can be substituted for unit tests without touching any business logic.

All methods are synchronous and blocking. The coordinator and Automation Hub call these from their main processing threads — not from Netty I/O threads — so blocking is acceptable.

Implementations must be thread-safe. The coordinator's Netty server may serve concurrent requests from the API Gateway on multiple threads.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest claimRequest)
    Creates a pending device record before the physical device has connected.
    void
    close()
    Closes the store cleanly — flushes pending writes, closes connections.
    void
    deleteDevice(String deviceId)
    Removes a device and all its supported actions from the store.
    void
    deleteRule(String ruleId)
    Removes an automation rule from the store.
    void
    deleteUser(String userId)
    Deletes a user and cascades — removes all devices and rules owned by that user.
    List<AutomationRule>
    getAllActiveRules()
    Returns all active automation rules across all users.
    List<Device>
    getAllDevices()
    Returns all devices registered in the system across all users.
    List<AutomationRule>
    getAllRules()
    Returns all automation rules across all users.
    List<User>
    getAllUsers()
    Returns all users registered in the system.
    Optional<Device>
    getDeviceById(String deviceId)
    Returns the device with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
    List<Device>
    getDevicesByUser(String userId)
    Returns all devices registered by the given user.
    Optional<AutomationRule>
    getRuleById(String ruleId)
    Returns the automation rule with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
    List<AutomationRule>
    getRulesByUser(String userId)
    Returns all automation rules owned by the given user.
    Optional<User>
    getUserById(String userId)
    Returns the user with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
    Optional<User>
    getUserByUsername(String username)
    Returns the user with the given username, or Optional.empty() if not found.
    void
    init()
    Initialises the store — creates tables if they do not exist, opens connection pools, etc.
    Map<String,String>
    loadStateForDevice(String deviceId)
     
    void
    saveDevice(Device device)
    Persists a newly registered device and its supported actions.
    void
    saveRule(AutomationRule rule)
    Persists a new automation rule.
    void
    saveUser(User user)
    Persists a new user.
    void
    setRuleActive(String ruleId, boolean active)
    Updates the active/inactive flag of an existing rule.
    void
    updateDeviceOnlineStatus(String deviceId, boolean online)
    Updates the online/offline status of a device.
    void
    updateDeviceState(String deviceId, Map<String,String> state)
    Updates the persistent state for a device.

  • Method Details

    • init

      void init()
      Initialises the store — creates tables if they do not exist, opens connection pools, etc. Called once during component startup before any other method is invoked.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the store cannot be initialised

    • close

      void close()
      Closes the store cleanly — flushes pending writes, closes connections. Called during component shutdown.

    • saveUser

      void saveUser(User user)
      Persists a new user. Throws if a user with the same ID or username already exists.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to save; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a user with the same ID or username exists

    • getUserById

      Optional<User> getUserById(String userId)
      Returns the user with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Parameters:
      userId - the ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching user, or empty

    • getUserByUsername

      Optional<User> getUserByUsername(String username)
      Returns the user with the given username, or Optional.empty() if not found. Used during login / request authentication.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      Returns:
      the matching user, or empty

    • getAllUsers

      List<User> getAllUsers()
      Returns all users registered in the system.
      Returns:
      list of all users; empty list if none exist

    • deleteUser

      void deleteUser(String userId)
      Deletes a user and cascades — removes all devices and rules owned by that user.
      Parameters:
      userId - the ID of the user to delete

    • saveDevice

      void saveDevice(Device device)
      Persists a newly registered device and its supported actions. If a device with the same ID already exists, updates it (upsert).
      Parameters:
      device - the device to save; must not be null

    • claimDevice

      void claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest claimRequest)
      Creates a pending device record before the physical device has connected. Called when a user claims a device in the app by providing its ID, name, and type. The record is stored with online=false and no actions (capabilities are unknown until the device connects and sends its RegistrationPayload).

      If a record for this device ID already exists (device connected before the user claimed it), updates the existing record with the userId and name rather than inserting a duplicate.

      Parameters:
      claimRequest - the claim request containing user ID, device ID, name, and type

    • getDeviceById

      Optional<Device> getDeviceById(String deviceId)
      Returns the device with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the device ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching device, or empty

    • getDevicesByUser

      List<Device> getDevicesByUser(String userId)
      Returns all devices registered by the given user.
      Parameters:
      userId - the user whose devices to retrieve
      Returns:
      list of devices owned by this user; empty list if none

    • getAllDevices

      List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Returns all devices registered in the system across all users. Used by the coordinator on startup to rebuild its in-memory device registry.
      Returns:
      list of all devices; empty list if none

    • updateDeviceOnlineStatus

      void updateDeviceOnlineStatus(String deviceId, boolean online)
      Updates the online/offline status of a device. Called by the coordinator's heartbeat monitor when a device's liveness changes — does not affect any other device fields.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to update
      online - true to mark online, false to mark offline

    • updateDeviceState

      void updateDeviceState(String deviceId, Map<String,String> state)
      Updates the persistent state for a device.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to update
      state - a map of key-value pairs representing the device's state

    • loadStateForDevice

      Map<String,String> loadStateForDevice(String deviceId) throws SQLException
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • deleteDevice

      void deleteDevice(String deviceId)
      Removes a device and all its supported actions from the store.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to delete

    • saveRule

      void saveRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Persists a new automation rule. If a rule with the same ID already exists, updates it (upsert).
      Parameters:
      rule - the rule to save; must not be null

    • getRuleById

      Optional<AutomationRule> getRuleById(String ruleId)
      Returns the automation rule with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the rule ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching rule, or empty

    • getRulesByUser

      List<AutomationRule> getRulesByUser(String userId)
      Returns all automation rules owned by the given user. Includes both active and inactive rules.
      Parameters:
      userId - the user whose rules to retrieve
      Returns:
      list of rules owned by this user; empty list if none

    • getAllActiveRules

      List<AutomationRule> getAllActiveRules()
      Returns all active automation rules across all users. Used by the Automation Hub on startup to load its working rule set without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      Returns:
      list of all active rules; empty list if none

    • getAllRules

      List<AutomationRule> getAllRules()
      Returns all automation rules across all users. Used by the Automation Hub on startup to load its working rule set without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      Returns:
      list of all active rules; empty list if none

    • setRuleActive

      void setRuleActive(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Updates the active/inactive flag of an existing rule. Called by toggle operations — does not affect any other rule fields.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to update
      active - true to activate, false to deactivate

    • deleteRule

      void deleteRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an automation rule from the store.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to delete
